# ---- Substrate: read the already-cleaned CSV from data/ ----
# substrate_cleaned.csv was produced by the original analysis.R cleaning pipeline.
# It already has abiotic_prop, algae_prop, sponge_prop, coral_prop, other_prop
# and all individual substrate columns. We just add pct columns + time fields.
substrate_clean <- read_csv("data/substrate_cleaned.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  mutate(
    date       = lubridate::as_date(lubridate::parse_date_time(date,
                   orders = c("ymd", "dmy", "mdy", "ymd HMS", "dmy HMS"))),
    # Fix 3: str_trim before any site matching
    dive_site  = str_trim(dive_site),
    # Recompute proportions with corrected groupings from raw columns.
    # abiotic = si + sa + rb + rk (hard substrate only)
    # other_benthic = oc + bc + fc + an + cm + tu + bcm + unknown
    abiotic_pts       = rowSums(across(any_of(abiotic_cols)),       na.rm = TRUE),
    other_benthic_pts = rowSums(across(any_of(other_benthic_cols)), na.rm = TRUE) +
                        coalesce(unknown, 0L),
    coral_pct         = coral_prop * 100,
    algae_pct         = algae_prop * 100,
    sponge_pct        = sponge_prop * 100,
    abiotic_pct       = (abiotic_pts / total_data_points) * 100,
    other_benthic_pct = (other_benthic_pts / total_data_points) * 100,
    # recompute props to match (used in PERMANOVA community matrix)
    abiotic_prop       = abiotic_pct / 100,
    other_benthic_prop = other_benthic_pct / 100,
    mean_depth = (depth_start + depth_end) / 2,
    year       = as.integer(lubridate::year(date)),
    month_year = format(date, "%b %Y"),
    dive_site  = factor(dive_site, levels = site_levels)
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(dive_site))
